verses.text: A great drought parches the land; nobles send servants for water who return with empty vessels, ashamed. The dearth is severe. Withheld rain is God's call to a people who forgot Him.
A great drought parches the land; nobles send servants for water who return with empty vessels, ashamed. The dearth is severe. Withheld rain is God's call to a people who forgot Him.
A great drought parches the land; nobles send servants for water who return with empty vessels, ashamed. The dearth is severe. Withheld rain is God's call to a people who forgot Him.
The ground is cracked, the plowmen ashamed, even the wild beasts panting and failing for lack of grass. All creation suffers. The land itself groans under the sin of its people.
The ground is cracked, the plowmen ashamed, even the wild beasts panting and failing for lack of grass. All creation suffers. The land itself groans under the sin of its people.

Jeremiah prays, 'though our iniquities testify against us, do thou it for thy name's sake,' for God is in their midst. He pleads not their worth but God's honour. The surest ground of prayer is God's own name.

Jeremiah prays, 'though our iniquities testify against us, do thou it for thy name's sake,' for God is in their midst. He pleads not their worth but God's honour. The surest ground of prayer is God's own name.

Jeremiah prays, 'though our iniquities testify against us, do thou it for thy name's sake,' for God is in their midst. He pleads not their worth but God's honour. The surest ground of prayer is God's own name.

But God will not accept them now; their fasting and offerings He will not hear, for He will consume them by sword, famine, and pestilence. Judgment is fixed. Empty ritual cannot turn aside the wrath their sin has earned.

Jeremiah blames the false prophets who promised peace; God answers that they and their hearers shall fall by sword and famine. Lying assurance is deadly. Those who flatter a sinful people share their doom.

Jeremiah blames the false prophets who promised peace; God answers that they and their hearers shall fall by sword and famine. Lying assurance is deadly. Those who flatter a sinful people share their doom.
Jeremiah weeps day and night for 'the virgin daughter of my people' broken with a grievous wound. His grief overflows. The true prophet mourns the ruin he must announce.
Jeremiah weeps day and night for 'the virgin daughter of my people' broken with a grievous wound. His grief overflows. The true prophet mourns the ruin he must announce.

He confesses, 'We acknowledge... our wickedness,' and pleads, 'do not abhor us, for thy name's sake'; only God sends rain. Penitent prayer clings to God. When all hope fails, the soul casts itself on His covenant mercy.
